items.title: Set theme once per notebook or script
items.avoid: Mixing default, dark, and whitegrid styles across figures in the same report.
items.prefer: Call `sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid", context="notebook")` at the start for consistent fonts, grid, and colors.
items.why: Visual consistency helps readers compare charts; theme changes mid-notebook look unpolished.
items.example: Add `sns.set_theme()` in the first cell before any plot calls in a Jupyter EDA notebook.

items.title: Select numeric columns before correlation heatmaps
items.avoid: Including categorical columns encoded as strings or arbitrary integer codes in `df.corr()`.
items.prefer: Use `df.select_dtypes(include='number')` or explicitly list numeric columns; consider Spearman for monotonic non-linear ties.
items.why: Pearson correlation on mis-typed or ordinal codes produces misleading coefficients.

items.title: Use data= and column names instead of positional arrays
items.avoid: Passing bare arrays when a DataFrame is available— loses self-documenting axis labels.
items.prefer: Write `sns.scatterplot(data=df, x='feature_a', y='feature_b', hue='label')` so column names appear in legends and code reviews.
items.why: Named columns survive refactors and match how pandas-centric ML pipelines store features.
items.example: Replace `sns.scatterplot(x=df['a'], y=df['b'])` with the `data=` form for clearer faceting later.

items.title: Limit pair plot dimensionality
items.avoid: Running `pairplot` on every column of a wide table.
items.prefer: Pick ≤6 informative numeric features; use `corner=True`, `diag_kind='hist'`, and `hue` for class color.
items.why: Readable pair plots focus attention; exhaustive grids obscure signal.
items.example: `sns.pairplot(df[cols], hue='target', corner=True, plot_kws={'alpha':0.4})` with cols chosen from domain or variance.
